149	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
150	        applyCards(prev => {
151	          let existingComments = []
152	          let found = false
153	          const next = {}
154	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
155	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
156	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
157	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
158	          }
159	          if (!found) return prev
160	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
161	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
162	          return next
163	        })
164	        break
165	      }
166	      case 'card:deleted': {
167	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
168	        applyCards(prev => {
169	          const next = {}
170	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
171	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
172	          }
173	          return next
174	        })
175	        break
176	      }
177	      case 'card:moved': {
178	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
179	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
180	        applyCards(prev => {
181	          const next = {}
182	          let existingComments = []
183	          let found = false
184	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
185	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
186	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
187	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
188	          }
189	          if (!found) return prev
190	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
191	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
192	          return next
193	        })
194	        break
195	      }
196	      case 'comment:created': {
197	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
198	        applyCards(prev => {
199	          const next = {}
200	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
201	            next[k] = col.map(c =>
202	              c.id === payload.card_id
203	                ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, payload] }
204	                : c
205	            )
206	          }
207	          return next
208	        })
209	        break
210	      }
211	    }
212	  }, [])
213	
214	  const { status: wsStatus } = useWebSocket(WS_URL, { onEvent: handleWsEvent, events: WS_EVENTS })
215	
216	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	  // Reconnect reconciliation — re-fetch on WS reconnect to recover missed events
218	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	  useEffect(() => {
221	    if (wsStatus === 'connected') {
222	      if (hasConnectedRef.current) {
223	        // Reconnect detected — re-fetch to reconcile any missed events
224	        let cancelled = false
225	        fetchCards()
226	          .then(data => { if (!cancelled) applyCards(groupCards(data)) })
227	          .catch(() => {}) // silent fail; don't disrupt existing state
228	        return () => { cancelled = true }
229	      }
230	      hasConnectedRef.current = true
231	    }
232	  }, [wsStatus])
233	
234	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	  // Initial data load
236	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
237	
238	  useEffect(() => {
239	    let cancelled = false
240	    setError(null)
241	    beginOp()
242	
243	    fetchCards()
244	      .then(data => {
245	        if (cancelled) return
246	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
247	        endOp()
248	      })
249	      .catch(err => {
250	        if (cancelled) return
251	        setError(err.message)
252	        endOp()
253	      })
254	
255	    return () => { cancelled = true }
256	  }, [])
257	
258	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
260	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
263	    const tempId = nextTempId()
264	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
265	    const key = columnToKey(col)
266	
267	    const optimisticCard = {
268	      id: tempId,
269	      title: data.title,
270	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
271	      column: col,
272	      position: Infinity,
273	      description: data.description ?? null,
274	      created_at: Date.now(),
275	      comments: [],
276	    }
277	
278	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
279	    beginOp()
280	
281	    try {
282	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
283	      suppressWsEvent(created.id)
284	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
285	      applyCards(prev => {
286	        const next = { ...prev }
287	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
288	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
289	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
290	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
291	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
292	        return next
293	      })
294	      return created
295	    } catch (err) {
296	      applyCards(prev => ({
297	        ...prev,
298	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
299	      }))
300	      throw err
301	    } finally {
302	      endOp()
303	    }
304	  }, [])
305	
306	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
307	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
308	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
309	
310	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
311	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
312	    const safeData = { ...data }
313	    delete safeData.column
314	
315	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
316	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
317	    suppressWsEvent(id)
318	    applyCards(prev => {
319	      const next = {}
320	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
321	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
322	      }
323	      return next
324	    })
325	    beginOp()
326	
327	    try {
328	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
329	      applyCards(prev => {
330	        const next = {}
331	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
332	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
333	        }
334	        return next
335	      })
336	      return updated
337	    } catch (err) {
338	      applyCards(rollback)
339	      throw err
340	    } finally {
341	      endOp()
342	    }
343	  }, [])
344	
345	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
346	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
347	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
348	
349	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
350	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
351	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
352	    suppressWsEvent(id)
353	    applyCards(prev => {
354	      const next = {}
355	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
356	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
357	      }
358	      return next
359	    })
360	    beginOp()
361	
362	    try {
363	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
364	    } catch (err) {
365	      applyCards(rollback)
366	      throw err
367	    } finally {
368	      endOp()
369	    }
370	  }, [])
371	
372	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
373	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
374	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
375	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
376	
377	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
378	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
379	
380	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
381	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
382	    suppressWsEvent(id)
383	    applyCards(prev => {
384	      let cardToMove = null
385	      const next = {}
386	
387	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
388	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
389	        if (idx !== -1) {
390	          cardToMove = col[idx]
391	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
392	        } else {
393	          next[k] = col
394	        }
395	      }
396	
397	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
398	
399	      const movedCard = {
400	        ...cardToMove,
401	        column: targetColumn,
402	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
403	      }
404	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
405	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
406	      return next
407	    })
408	    beginOp()
409	
410	    try {
411	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
412	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
413	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
414	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
415	      applyCards(prev => {
416	        const next = {}
417	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
418	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
419	        }
420	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
421	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
422	        return next
423	      })
424	      return updated
425	    } catch (err) {
426	      applyCards(rollback)
427	      throw err
428	    } finally {
429	      endOp()
430	    }
431	  }, [])
432	
433	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
435	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
436	
437	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
438	    const tempId = nextTempId()
439	    const optimisticComment = {
440	      id: tempId,
441	      card_id: cardId,
442	      author: data.author,
443	      content: data.content,
444	      created_at: Date.now(),
445	    }
446	
447	    applyCards(prev => {
448	      const next = {}
449	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
450	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
451	          c.id === cardId
452	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
453	            : c
454	        )
455	      }
456	      return next
457	    })
458	    beginOp()
459	
460	    try {
461	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
462	      suppressWsEvent(comment.id)
463	      applyCards(prev => {
464	        const next = {}
465	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
466	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
467	            c.id === cardId
468	              ? {
469	                  ...c,
470	                  comments: c.comments
471	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
472	                    .concat(comment),
473	                }
474	              : c
475	          )
476	        }
477	        return next
478	      })
479	      return comment
480	    } catch (err) {
481	      applyCards(prev => {
482	        const next = {}
483	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
484	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
485	            c.id === cardId
486	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
487	              : c
488	          )
489	        }
490	        return next
491	      })
492	      throw err
493	    } finally {
494	      endOp()
495	    }
496	  }, [])
497	
498	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
499	  // Public interface
500	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
501	
502	  return {
503	    cards,
504	    loading,
505	    error,
506	    createCard,
507	    updateCard,
508	    deleteCard,
509	    moveCard,
510	    addComment,
511	  }
512	}

1	/**
2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}

The return JSX wraps everything in: ```jsx ... ... ... ``` Sensors are configured with appropriate activation constraints: - `MouseSensor`: `{ activationConstraint: { distance: 8 } }` (prevents accidental drags on click) - `TouchSensor`: `{ activationConstraint: { delay: 200, tolerance: 5 } }` (prevents conflicts with scroll) All hooks (`useSensors`, `useSensor`) are declared before any early returns, satisfying React's Rules of Hooks. --- ### 4.3 Column — SortableContext **Requirement:** Make Column components use `SortableContext` **Status: Correct** `Column.jsx` imports `SortableContext`, `verticalListSortingStrategy` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` and `useDroppable` from `@dnd-kit/core`. ```jsx const { setNodeRef, isOver } = useDroppable({ id: columnId }) const cardIds = cards.map(c => c.id)
...
``` The droppable `ref` is attached to `column-cards` div (inside `SortableContext`). The column itself uses `isOver` to conditionally apply the `column-drag-over` CSS class, providing visual feedback when a card is hovered over the column. --- ### 4.4 CardTile — useSortable Hook **Requirement:** Add `useSortable` hook to `CardTile` components **Status: Correct** `CardTile.jsx` imports `useSortable` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` and `CSS` from `@dnd-kit/utilities`: ```jsx const { attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition, isDragging } = useSortable({ id: card.id }) const style = { transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform), transition, } ``` The card div receives `ref={setNodeRef}`, `style={style}`, and spreads `{...attributes}` and `{...restListeners}`. The `isDragging` flag adds a `card-tile-dragging` CSS class. One implementation nuance worth noting: the code destructures `onKeyDown` from `listeners` to compose it with the inline editing keyboard handler: ```jsx const { onKeyDown: dndKeyDown, ...restListeners } = listeners ?? {} ``` This correctly handles the case where `listeners` may be `null`/`undefined` (via the `?? {}` fallback). The composed handler suppresses DnD keyboard events while a field is in edit mode, preventing conflicts between edit mode and DnD keyboard navigation. --- ### 4.5 onDragEnd Handler **Requirement:** Implement `onDragEnd` handler to call `moveCard` API; handle both cross-column moves and within-column reordering **Status: Correct** `Board.jsx` implements `handleDragEnd` with the following logic: **Guard conditions:** - Returns early if `over` is null (dropped outside any droppable) - Returns early if `active.id === over.id` (dropped on itself) - Returns early if `sourceColumn` is not found - Returns early if `targetColumn` is not found - Returns early for same-column header drop (no-op) **Cross-column move:** Uses `calculatePosition()` exported pure helper to compute fractional position. Filters out the dragged card from target column before computing the insert index. Falls back to appending if `overId` is a column ID (dropped on empty column header). **Within-column reorder:** Uses `arrayMove()` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` to compute the new order, then re-derives `position` as the midpoint of neighbors: ```js const before = reordered[newIndex - 1]?.position ?? 0 const after = reordered[newIndex + 1]?.position const newPosition = after !== undefined ? No server test was broken or regressed by this task. --- ## 4. Implementation Review ### 4.1 Package Installation **Requirement:** Install `@dnd-kit/core`, `@dnd-kit/sortable`, `@dnd-kit/utilities` **Status: Correct** All three packages are present in `client/package.json` under `dependencies`: - `@dnd-kit/core@^6.1.0` (installed: 6.3.1) - `@dnd-kit/sortable@^8.0.0` (installed: 8.0.0) - `@dnd-kit/utilities@^3.2.2` (installed: 3.2.2) Confirmed with `npm ls @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable @dnd-kit/utilities`. --- ### 4.2 Board — DndContext Wrapping **Requirement:** Wrap Board in `DndContext` with `closestCenter` collision detection **Status: Correct** `Board.jsx` imports `DndContext`, `closestCenter`, `DragOverlay`, `useSensor`, `useSensors`, `MouseSensor`, `TouchSensor` from `@dnd-kit/core`. The return JSX wraps everything in: ```jsx ... ... ... ``` Sensors are configured with appropriate activation constraints: - `MouseSensor`: `{ activationConstraint: { distance: 8 } }` (prevents accidental drags on click) - `TouchSensor`: `{ activationConstraint: { delay: 200, tolerance: 5 } }` (prevents conflicts with scroll) All hooks (`useSensors`, `useSensor`) are declared before any early returns, satisfying React's Rules of Hooks. --- ### 4.3 Column — SortableContext **Requirement:** Make Column components use `SortableContext` **Status: Correct** `Column.jsx` imports `SortableContext`, `verticalListSortingStrategy` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` and `useDroppable` from `@dnd-kit/core`. ```jsx const { setNodeRef, isOver } = useDroppable({ id: columnId }) const cardIds = cards.map(c => c.id)
...
``` The droppable `ref` is attached to `column-cards` div (inside `SortableContext`). The column itself uses `isOver` to conditionally apply the `column-drag-over` CSS class, providing visual feedback when a card is hovered over the column. --- ### 4.4 CardTile — useSortable Hook **Requirement:** Add `useSortable` hook to `CardTile` components **Status: Correct** `CardTile.jsx` imports `useSortable` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` and `CSS` from `@dnd-kit/utilities`: ```jsx const { attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition, isDragging } = useSortable({ id: card.id }) const style = { transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform), transition, } ``` The card div receives `ref={setNodeRef}`, `style={style}`, and spreads `{...attributes}` and `{...restListeners}`. The `isDragging` flag adds a `card-tile-dragging` CSS class. One implementation nuance worth noting: the code destructures `onKeyDown` from `listeners` to compose it with the inline editing keyboard handler: ```jsx const { onKeyDown: dndKeyDown, ...restListeners } = listeners ?? {} ``` This correctly handles the case where `listeners` may be `null`/`undefined` (via the `?? {}` fallback). The composed handler suppresses DnD keyboard events while a field is in edit mode, preventing conflicts between edit mode and DnD keyboard navigation. --- ### 4.5 onDragEnd Handler **Requirement:** Implement `onDragEnd` handler to call `moveCard` API; handle both cross-column moves and within-column reordering **Status: Correct** `Board.jsx` implements `handleDragEnd` with the following logic: **Guard conditions:** - Returns early if `over` is null (dropped outside any droppable) - Returns early if `active.id === over.id` (dropped on itself) - Returns early if `sourceColumn` is not found - Returns early if `targetColumn` is not found - Returns early for same-column header drop (no-op) **Cross-column move:** Uses `calculatePosition()` exported pure helper to compute fractional position. Filters out the dragged card from target column before computing the insert index. Falls back to appending if `overId` is a column ID (dropped on empty column header). **Within-column reorder:** Uses `arrayMove()` from `@dnd-kit/sortable` to compute the new order, then re-derives `position` as the midpoint of neighbors: ```js const before = reordered[newIndex - 1]?.position ?? 0 const after = reordered[newIndex + 1]?.position const newPosition = after !== undefined ? (before + after) / 2 : before + 1 ``` In both cases, `moveCard(activeId, targetColumn, newPosition)` is called, which triggers the optimistic state update and API PATCH call in `useBoard.js`. **Observation — moveCard API call:** The task requirement states "call moveCard API". The implementation calls `useBoard`'s `moveCard` function, which internally calls `apiUpdateCard` with `{ column, position }` via `PATCH /api/cards/:id`. This is architecturally correct — there is no dedicated `moveCard` REST endpoint; moves go through the generic update endpoint. --- ### 4.6 Visual Feedback During Drag **Requirement:** Add visual feedback during drag with CSS transforms **Status: Correct** Three layers of visual feedback are implemented: 1. **CSS transform on dragged card** — `CardTile` applies `transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform)` and `transition` as inline styles. dnd-kit updates `transform` in real-time as the user drags, smoothly moving the card element. 2. **Dragging placeholder** — When `isDragging` is true, `.card-tile-dragging` is applied: ```css .card-tile-dragging { opacity: 0.3; background: #d0d4e0; box-shadow: none; transform: none !important; } ``` This leaves a faded placeholder in the card's original position. 3. **DragOverlay** — `Board.jsx` renders a `DragOverlay` that shows a floating ghost card during drag. The `handleDragStart` handler sets `activeCard` state; the overlay renders the card's title, assignee, and description. The overlay card has `.card-drag-overlay` CSS: ```css .card-drag-overlay { opacity: 0.95; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25); cursor: grabbing; transform: rotate(1.5deg); } ``` 4. **Column drag-over highlight** — When a card is dragged over a column, `.column-drag-over` applies a blue dashed outline: ```css .column-drag-over { background: #d8dff0; outline: 2px dashed #4a6fa5; outline-offset: -2px; } ``` --- ### 4.7 Pure Utility Functions (Exported for Testing) Two pure helpers are exported from `Board.jsx`: **`findCardColumn(cardId, cards)`** — Iterates over the cards state object to find which column contains a given card ID. Normalizes `in_progress` state key back to `in-progress` API format. Returns `null` if not found. **`calculatePosition(sortedCards, insertIndex)`** — Computes fractional position for cross-column drops: returns 1.0 for empty columns, midpoint between neighbors when inserting between cards, or `lastPosition + 1` when appending. Both are tested directly via dynamic `import('./Board.jsx')` in `Board.test.jsx`. --- ### 4.8 Code Quality Observations 1. **`useBoard.moveCard` call correctness:** The `handleDragEnd` function calls `moveCard(activeId, targetColumn, newPosition)` where `targetColumn` is always in API format (`'ready'`, `'in-progress'`, `'done'`). The `useBoard.moveCard` function documents that it expects API format. This is consistent. 2. **Edge case — same-column reorder with `oldIndex === newIndex`:** The handler correctly guards with `if (oldIndex === -1 || newIndex === -1 || oldIndex === newIndex) return` — no unnecessary API call is made when a card is dropped back in its original position. 3. **`listeners ?? {}`:** This guards against `listeners` being `null` when `useSortable` is mocked or when the sortable context is not active. 4. **Position arithmetic:** The position calculation uses fractional midpoint bisection (e.g., `(2 + 4) / 2 = 3`). Over many reorders this can lead to precision exhaustion, but this is an accepted limitation of the fractional indexing pattern at this scale. 5. **No TypeScript / no prop-types:** Consistent with the project's established convention — eslint config explicitly disables `react/prop-types`. Not a defect. --- ## 5.
